turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="sky" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#ffefdb"/>
      <stop offset="60%" stop-color="#ffd59a"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ffe1b3"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="sunGlow" cx="360" cy="120" r="120">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#ffd27f"/>
      <stop offset="60%" stop-color="#ffb84d" stop-opacity="0.9"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ff8c00" stop-opacity="0.6"/>
    </radialGradient>
  </defs>
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="url(#sky)"/>
  <circle cx="360" cy="120" r="90" fill="url(#sunGlow)"/>
  <g fill="#ffd27f" opacity="0.6">
    <polygon points="360,40 380,120 360,100 340,120"/>
    <polygon points="430,120 370,120 405,150"/>
    <polygon points="300,100 320,120 300,140 290,120"/>
    <polygon points="430,80 410,110 440,110"/>
  </g>
  <polygon points="0,420 160,380 200,360 260,340 320,360 420,380 512,420 512,512 0,512" fill="#5d4037"/>
  <path d="M0 420 L160 380 L200 360 L260 348 L320 360 L420 380" fill="#4e342e" opacity="0.6"/>
  <g transform="translate(220,250) scale(1.1)">
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="100" rx="48" ry="14" fill="#000" opacity="0.25"/>
    <path d="M-18 -44 C-140 -30 -120 70 -10 86 L4 24 C12 -6 8 -28 -18 -44 Z" fill="#c0392b"/>
    <path d="M-12 -42 C-6 -30 6 -30 12 -42 L12 12 C6 20 -6 20 -12 12 Z" fill="#2c3e50"/>
    <circle cx="0" cy="-62" r="16" fill="#2c3e50"/>
    <path d="M-8 12 L-12 72 L4 72 L4 60 L-4 60 L-2 20 Z" fill="#2c3e50"/>
    <path d="M12 12 L16 72 L30 68 L22 60 L18 36 Z" fill="#2c3e50"/>
    <path d="M12 -10 L36 -44 L40 -40 L18 -6 Z" fill="#2c3e50"/>
    <g transform="translate(42,-48) rotate(-20)">
      <rect x="-6" y="-2" width="60" height="6" rx="2" fill="#bdc3c7"/>
      <rect x="-8" y="0" width="8" height="14" rx="2" fill="#7f8c8d"/>
      <polygon points="52,3 66,0 52,-3" fill="#ecf0f1"/>
    </g>
    <path d="M-6 -10 C0 -2 6 -2 10 -10" stroke="#34495e" stroke-width="2" fill="none" opacity="0.5"/>
  </g>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

I'm considering symbols like a shining heart, a lion, or maybe a person standing on a cliff during sunrise. A shield with a sword is another option. It should be minimalistic, so I might depict a figure in a heroic pose on a cliff with a cape blowing in the wind and rays from the sun behind. The design should focus on using simple shapes without scripts or foreignObjects.**Creating SVG JSON for Bravery**\n\nI’m considering the design for an SVG to represent bravery. I could do a silhouette of a person on a jagged cliff with a raised sword and sun rays behind. Using warm colors like oranges would evoke bravery nicely. I'll make sure there's no text in the SVG but will return it in a JSON format. The JSON will include the SVG string, ensuring all quotes are properly escaped. Let's structure it as \"svg\": \"<svg ...>...</svg>\" while taking care to maintain the correct formatting.**Setting Up SVG Elements**\n\nI'm planning the layout for my SVG. The sun will be in the top right, and I'll create rays as low-opacity paths. The cliff will be defined using polygon coordinates for the rock base. For the person’s silhouette, I'll use a circle for the head, a rounded path for the body, and a long rectangle for the sword arm. The cape will be a red polygon blowing in the wind, plus a shadow ellipse under the feet. I'll make sure to keep everything simple and without text.**Creating SVG JSON Markup**\n\nI’m outlining the details for my SVG design. The body will be a path going from the neck down to the waist, with specific coordinates. For the legs, I’ll simplify it with a differentiating path. The sword will be represented as a narrow rectangle, and the cape will be a polygon behind the body. I'll include a shadow ellipse under the feet with a black fill. Lastly, I’ll define the colors clearly and produce the JSON with escaped quotes for the SVG markup. message.reasoning:
**Designing an SVG for Bravery**

I need to create an SVG drawing symbolizing "bravery" without using text, numbers, or letters. I'm considering symbols like a shining heart, a lion, or maybe a person standing on a cliff during sunrise. A shield with a sword is another option. It should be minimalistic, so I might depict a figure in a heroic pose on a cliff with a cape blowing in the wind and rays from the sun behind. The design should focus on using simple shapes without scripts or foreignObjects.**Creating SVG JSON for Bravery**

I’m considering the design for an SVG to represent bravery. I could do a silhouette of a person on a jagged cliff with a raised sword and sun rays behind. Using warm colors like oranges would evoke bravery nicely. I'll make sure there's no text in the SVG but will return it in a JSON format. The JSON will include the SVG string, ensuring all quotes are properly escaped. Let's structure it as "svg": "<svg ...>...</svg>" while taking care to maintain the correct formatting.**Setting Up SVG Elements**

I'm planning the layout for my SVG. The sun will be in the top right, and I'll create rays as low-opacity paths. The cliff will be defined using polygon coordinates for the rock base. For the person’s silhouette, I'll use a circle for the head, a rounded path for the body, and a long rectangle for the sword arm. The cape will be a red polygon blowing in the wind, plus a shadow ellipse under the feet. I'll make sure to keep everything simple and without text.**Creating SVG JSON Markup**

I’m outlining the details for my SVG design. The body will be a path going from the neck down to the waist, with specific coordinates. For the legs, I’ll simplify it with a differentiating path. The sword will be represented as a narrow rectangle, and the cape will be a polygon behind the body. I'll include a shadow ellipse under the feet with a black fill. Lastly, I’ll define the colors clearly and produce the JSON with escaped quotes for the SVG markup. No text will be included, of course!**Finalizing SVG JSON**

I’m putting the finishing touches on my SVG code. It contains elements like a gradient background, the sun, cliff, and hero silhouette. While I notice there are digits in the attributes, they’re necessary, and it seems my focus should be on ensuring there’s no visible text or characters in the drawing itself. I’ll work on producing the final JSON string, escaping the quotes correctly while keeping the newlines. It's essential to have everything properly formatted in the JSON object!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}

response.rawBody: {"id":"gen-1772945594-oUU0ZmYv51l60yqvDb8d","object":"chat.completion","created":1772945594,"model":"openai/gpt-5-mini-2025-08-07","provider":"Azure","system_fingerprint":null,"choices":[{"index":0,"logprobs":null,"finish_reason":"stop","native_finish_reason":"completed","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"{\"guesses\":[\"hero\",\"warrior\",\"knight\",\"cape\",\"sword\",\"battle\",\"quest\",\"conquer\",\"brave\",\"victory\",\"sun\",\"sunrise\",\"sunset\",\"dune\",\"hill\",\"lone\",\"silhouette\",\"adventurer\",\"epic\",\"champion\"]}","refusal":null,"reasoning":"**Creating word list**\n\nI'm putting together a list of words, aiming for unique, one-word items related to themes like heroism and adventure.
